query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b139c14d).

## Issue to Resolve

Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

You're right. Can you provide a little more context where this variable is declared? Is it at top level, or in a companion object or class?
I have edited the issue, so you can see where the property is declared. 

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless {
 85→                // Allow
 86→                // object Foo {
 87→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88→                // }
 89→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91→            ?.let {
 92→                emit(
 93→                    identifier.startOffset,
 94→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95→                    false,
 96→                )
 97→            }
 98→    }
 99→
100→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101→        identifier: ASTNode,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        identifier
105→            .text
106→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107→            ?.let {
108→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109→            }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118→            ?.children()
119→            .orEmpty()
120→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129→            containsValKeyword() &&
130→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136→            ?.let { identifier ->
137→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138→            }
139→            ?: false
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142→        treeParent
143→            .children()
144→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146→            .any { it.text == identifier }
147→
148→    private companion object {
149→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153→    }
154→}
155→
156→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
157→

content.thinking:
**Revising naming rules for mutable collections**

I'm figuring out how to adjust the rule that enforces SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for constant properties so it doesn't wrongly apply to properties referencing mutable collections like MutableMap or MutableList. Since the reference itself is val but the collection contents can change, these should use lowerCamelCase naming. I plan to detect such mutable types by parsing the property’s type reference or initializer in the AST, checking for keywords like "Mutable", and will look for existing tests to inform the fix.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat
  4→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest
  7→import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.ValueSource
  8→
  9→class PropertyNamingRuleTest {
 10→    private val propertyNamingRuleAssertThat =
 11→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { PropertyNamingRule() }
 12→
 13→    @Test
 14→    fun `Given a valid property name then do not emit`() {
 15→        val code =
 16→            """
 17→            var foo = "foo"
 18→            """.trimIndent()
 19→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 20→    }
 21→
 22→    @ParameterizedTest(name = ": {0}")
 23→    @ValueSource(
 24→        strings = [
 25→            "Foo",
 26→            "foo_bar",
 27→        ],
 28→    )
 29→    fun `Given an invalid property name then do emit`(propertyName: String) {
 30→        val code =
 31→            """
 32→            var $propertyName = "foo"
 33→            """.trimIndent()
 34→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 35→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case")
 36→    }
 37→
 38→    @Test
 39→    fun `Given a const property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 40→        val code =
 41→            """
 42→            const val foo = "foo"
 43→            const val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar-2"
 44→            const val ŸÈŠ_THÎS_IS_ALLOWED_123 = "Yes this is allowed"
 45→            """.trimIndent()
 46→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 47→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 48→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 11, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    @Test
 52→    fun `Given a top level val property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 53→        val code =
 54→            """
 55→            val foo = Foo()
 56→            val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 57→            """.trimIndent()
 58→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 59→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 60→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    @Test
 64→    fun `Given an object val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 65→        val code =
 66→            """
 67→            class Foo {
 68→                companion object {
 69→                    val foo = Foo()
 70→                    val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→            """.trimIndent()
 74→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 75→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 76→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(3, 13, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    @Test
 80→    fun `Given an object override val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 81→        val code =
 82→            """
 83→            open class Foo {
 84→                open val foo = "foo"
 85→            }
 86→
 87→            val BAR = object : Foo() {
 88→                override val foo = "bar"
 89→            }
 90→            """.trimIndent()
 91→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    @Test
 95→    fun `Given an object val property name having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 96→        val code =
 97→            """
 98→            class Foo {
 99→                companion object {
100→                    val foo
101→                        get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
102→                }
103→            }
104→            """.trimIndent()
105→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
106→    }
107→
108→    @Test
109→    fun `Given a top level property extension function having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
110→        val code =
111→            """
112→            val fooBar1: Any
113→                get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
114→            val fooBar2
115→                inline get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
116→            val fooBar3
117→                @Bar get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
118→            """.trimIndent()
119→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
120→    }
121→
122→    @Test
123→    fun `Given a backing val property name having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
124→        val code =
125→            """
126→            class Foo {
127→                private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
128→
129→                val elementList: List<Element>
130→                    get() = _elementList
131→            }
132→            """.trimIndent()
133→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
134→    }
135→
136→    @Test
137→    fun `Given a backing val property name containing diacritics having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
138→        val code =
139→            """
140→            class Foo {
141→                private val _elementŁîšt = mutableListOf<Element>()
142→
143→                val elementŁîšt: List<Element>
144→                    get() = _elementList
145→            }
146→            """.trimIndent()
147→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
148→    }
149→
150→    @Test
151→    fun `Given a local variable then do not emit`() {
152→        val code =
153→            """
154→            fun foo() {
155→                val bar2 = "bar"
156→            }
157→            """.trimIndent()
158→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
159→    }
160→
161→    @ParameterizedTest(name = "Suppression annotation: {0}")
162→    @ValueSource(
163→        strings = [
164→            "ktlint:standard:property-naming",
165→            "PropertyName", // IntelliJ IDEA suppression
166→        ],
167→    )
168→    fun `Given class with a disallowed name which is suppressed`(suppressionName: String) {
169→        val code =
170→            """
171→            @Suppress("$suppressionName")
172→            val foo = Foo()
173→            """.trimIndent()
174→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
175→    }
176→
177→    @Nested
178→    inner class `Issue 2017 - Given property is serialVersionUID` {
179→        @Test
180→        fun `Given property is present in companion object`() {
181→            val code =
182→                """
183→                class Foo1 {
184→                    companion object {
185→                        private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
186→                    }
187→                }
188→                class Foo2 {
189→                    companion object {
190→                        private const val serialVersionUID = 123L
191→                    }
192→                }
193→                """.trimIndent()
194→            propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
195→        }
196→
197→        @Test
198→        fun `Given property defined object is private const`() {
199→            val code =
200→                """
201→                object Foo1 {
202→                    private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
203→                }
204→                object Foo2 {
205→                    private const val serialVersionUID = 123L
206→                }
207→                """.trimIndent()
208→            propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
209→        }
210→    }
211→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless {
 85→                // Allow
 86→                // object Foo {
 87→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88→                // }
 89→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91→            ?.let {
 92→                emit(
 93→                    identifier.startOffset,
 94→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95→                    false,
 96→                )
 97→            }
 98→    }
 99→
100→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101→        identifier: ASTNode,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        identifier
105→            .text
106→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107→            ?.let {
108→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109→            }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118→            ?.children()
119→            .orEmpty()
120→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129→            containsValKeyword() &&
130→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136→            ?.let { identifier ->
137→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138→            }
139→            ?: false
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142→        treeParent
143→            .children()
144→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146→            .any { it.text == identifier }
147→
148→    private companion object {
149→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153→    }
154→}
155→
156→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
157→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat
  4→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest
  7→import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.ValueSource
  8→
  9→class PropertyNamingRuleTest {
 10→    private val propertyNamingRuleAssertThat =
 11→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { PropertyNamingRule() }
 12→
 13→    @Test
 14→    fun `Given a valid property name then do not emit`() {
 15→        val code =
 16→            """
 17→            var foo = "foo"
 18→            """.trimIndent()
 19→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 20→    }
 21→
 22→    @ParameterizedTest(name = ": {0}")
 23→    @ValueSource(
 24→        strings = [
 25→            "Foo",
 26→            "foo_bar",
 27→        ],
 28→    )
 29→    fun `Given an invalid property name then do emit`(propertyName: String) {
 30→        val code =
 31→            """
 32→            var $propertyName = "foo"
 33→            """.trimIndent()
 34→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 35→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case")
 36→    }
 37→
 38→    @Test
 39→    fun `Given a const property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 40→        val code =
 41→            """
 42→            const val foo = "foo"
 43→            const val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar-2"
 44→            const val ŸÈŠ_THÎS_IS_ALLOWED_123 = "Yes this is allowed"
 45→            """.trimIndent()
 46→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 47→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 48→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 11, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    @Test
 52→    fun `Given a top level val property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 53→        val code =
 54→            """
 55→            val foo = Foo()
 56→            val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 57→            """.trimIndent()
 58→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 59→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 60→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    @Test
 64→    fun `Given an object val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 65→        val code =
 66→            """
 67→            class Foo {
 68→                companion object {
 69→                    val foo = Foo()
 70→                    val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→            """.trimIndent()
 74→        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 75→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 76→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(3, 13, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    @Test
 80→    fun `Given an object override val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 81→        val code =
 82→            """
 83→            open class Foo {
 84→                open val foo = "foo"
 85→            }
 86→
 87→            val BAR = object : Foo() {
 88→                override val foo = "bar"
 89→            }
 90→            """.trimIndent()
 91→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    @Test
 95→    fun `Given an object val property name having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 96→        val code =
 97→            """
 98→            class Foo {
 99→                companion object {
100→                    val foo
101→                        get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
102→                }
103→            }
104→            """.trimIndent()
105→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()

116→            val fooBar3
117→                @Bar get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
118→            """.trimIndent()
